pattern: Expanding around every possible center, both single characters and gaps between characters, counts all palindromic substrings in quadratic time.
hints: Every palindrome has a center, which is either one character or the gap between two characters.
From each center, expand outward while the mirrored characters match, counting one palindrome per successful expansion.
Loop over the 2n-1 centers, and for each grow left and right pointers while characters are equal, adding one to the count on each match.

edge_inputs.description: all distinct characters, only single-char palindromes
all same characters, many overlapping palindromes
no multi-char palindromes despite repeats far apart

stress.description: random short-alphabet string to create overlapping palindromes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    alpha = 'abc'
    return [''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n))]
